steps.title: Use the prediction clue
steps.content: The passage says the best way to predict how long something will continue to exist is to examine how long it has already survived. Therefore, an item's age is being described as something that points to its future duration.

steps.title: Match the precise noun
steps.content: A would make age a source of uncertainty, which reverses the passage. C makes age a motivation, and D makes it a criticism; neither fits prediction. The needed phrase means a sign or predictor of how much longer something will last. The answer is B.
